theme_and_archetype.reasoning: A fantasy football reference site requires maximum legibility, high data density, and clear visual hierarchy. The Swiss Brutalist/High-Contrast approach ensures tabular data is the hero, removing unnecessary decorative elements (like purple gradients) to create an authoritative, professional analytics tool.

typography.rules: Never use Inter for headings. Use JetBrains Mono for all numeric stats in tables to ensure perfect vertical alignment of digits.

spacing_and_layout.bento_spacing: Use tight gaps (gap-4) for data-heavy sections to mimic a control room, but generous padding (p-8) for overall page containers.

components.tables: Premium data-dense tables inspired by Pro Football Reference but modernized. Must have sticky headers with backdrop-blur-md (bg-white/90). Use subtle row striping (even:bg-slate-50). Hover state must highlight the entire row (hover:bg-slate-100). Use 1px border-slate-200 to separate columns slightly.
components.position_badges: Small pills (px-2.5 py-0.5 rounded-md text-xs font-bold 1px border) using the position_colors defined above. They immediately identify player role in dense lists.
components.cards: Flat solid background (bg-white), 1px border (border-slate-200), max 8px border-radius (rounded-md). No drop shadows by default. Focus on crisp, sharp edges.
components.buttons: Square or slight rounded-md. Primary buttons are solid black (bg-black text-white hover:bg-slate-800). Secondary are outline (border-slate-300 hover:bg-slate-50).
components.navigation: Solid white background with a crisp 1px bottom border. Clean, left-aligned logo. Horizontal nav items. No hamburgers on desktop.

visual_enhancers.borders: Grid borders ('The Technical Look'). Expose the skeleton for a data-first feel by keeping structural borders visible.
visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Restricted solely to sticky table headers or floating action bars to ensure underlying data remains somewhat visible on scroll but highly legible.
visual_enhancers.empty_states: Use the player action silhouette image as a subtle watermark or faint background element (opacity-10) for empty states or missing data.

hero_background.description: Stadium lights background. Use as a header banner for the main site or team pages, but MUST be covered with a heavy white overlay (bg-white/80 to bg-white/90) to keep high-contrast text perfectly legible.

player_action_silhouette.description: Silhouette player action. Ideal for player profile headers when a specific player photo is unavailable. Blend with multiply or high transparency.

instructions_to_main_agent: Install and configure 'Cabinet Grotesk' for headings, 'IBM Plex Sans' for body, and 'JetBrains Mono' for numeric table cells.
Install Phosphor Icons or Lucide-react for sharp, consistent UI icons.
Build a robust, filterable data table component mimicking Pro Football Reference. Include sortable column headers, sticky headers, and tabular numbers.
Implement the fantasy position color coding consistently across badges, charts, and row highlights. Do not use generic colors for positions.
Use Shadcn UI for complex interactive logic (Dropdowns for season/team filters, Tabs for passing/rushing/receiving stats) but customize styling to match the Swiss Brutalist flat/bordered aesthetic.
Integrate a 'Sleeper/Bust' visual indicator in the tables (e.g., an icon or subtle background tint on the specific cell).
Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='filter-season-2025').
CRITICAL: Avoid the 'AI-slop purple gradient' look entirely. Maintain strict adherence to the High-Contrast/Swiss monochrome palette with strategic position color highlights.
